author: Lachhman Prasad, Sufi
title: Azadi ka Raj
gloss: A pamphlet describing the blessings of the British rule and advising members of the various communities to cultivate friendly relate and thus benefit by the freedom granted under English Government.
notes: 'The Reign of Freedom' = British rule; free distribution. POLITICS section again wholly loyalist (cf. Q3) — the section's composition is itself the finding.

flags.issue: margin serial obscured by pencil X; read as 6 (follows s5 on p28, s7 follows)

author: Sukh Ram Das, Thakar
title: Hamara Farz-i-Ita'at
gloss: A tract in which the writer enumerates the blessings of the British rule in India and compares it favourably with those of the Muhammadans and of the Sikhs.
publisher: The author, who is Proprietor, 'Rajput Gazette'
notes: 'Our Duty of Obedience' by the Rajput Gazette proprietor — loyalism argued by ranking British rule above past Muslim and Sikh rule; communal comparison as political idiom.

author: Abdul Haq, Shaikh
title: Masail-ul-Libas
gloss: A book on dress as enjoined by the Muhammadan religion, translated into Urdu by Mirza Muhammad Nazir from the Persian original of Shah Abdul Haq of Delhi.
notes: Urdu—Religion serials resume at 80 from Q3. 'Abd al-Haqq Muhaddith Dihlawi (d. 1642) on lawful dress, Persian→Urdu — 17th-century sartorial fiqh revived for 1910 identity-marking debates. Dual date: 1328 Hijri / 15th August 1910.

author: Ahmad Ali, Mirza
title: Al-Insaf fil-Istikhlaf
gloss: A controversial book in which the writer tries to prove that Ali, the fourth Caliph, was the rightful successor to Muhammad.
notes: Shia succession apologetics; registrar labels it 'controversial' — the cataloguer's sectarian sensitivity index. Dual date: 1327 Hijri / October 1910.

author: Atma Ram, Shri
title: Daya Nand Kutark Timartarni
gloss: In which an attempt is made to refute the attacks made in the Satyarath Parkash of Swami Daya Nand, the founder of the Arya Samaj, on the religion of the Jains.
copyright: Lala Labhu Ram, District Ferozepore; No. 157, dated 25th October, 1910.
notes: Jain counter-polemic against the Satyarth Prakash, copyrighted — the smallest community in the polemic economy answering the loudest text; completes the quarter's ring: Arya vs Bible (p23 s155), Sikh shuddhi dialogue (p16 s15), Jain vs Arya here.

author: Bishan Das, Pandit
title: Qanuni-i-Karam
gloss: Or the law of Karma which teaches that our misfortunes in this world are due to our present and past actions, and that a perfectly blissful life is possible if a man does not tansgress the laws of Karma.
publisher: The Aryan Printing, Publishing and General Trading Company
copyright: The Aryan Printing and Publishing Company, Lahore; No. 180, dated the 21st November, 1910.
notes: Karma doctrine as corporate property — 'Printing, Publishing and General Trading Company' holds the copyright; catalog prints 'tansgress'.

title: Chaudhawin Sadi ke Masih ki ek Zinda Karamat
gloss: A pamphlet directed against Ghulam Ahmad of Qádián.
notes: 'A Living Miracle of the Fourteenth-Century Messiah' — sarcastic anti-Ahmadiyya polemic, free at 1,000 copies; sits in the same quarter as the Ahmadiyya's own annual report (p26 s180): movement and counter-movement in one catalog.
